summary: Matched the last two remaining functions in Yoshi's SpecialHi translation unit, bringing src/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialHi.c to 100%: 14/14 functions and 1836/1836 code bytes. The fixes were source-shape/codegen adjustments: an explicit upper clamp for stick-derived magnitude in ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C, removal of an incorrect PAD_STACK(4), and reuse of a scoped float temporary in fn_8012E110.
what_changed: Replaced a MAX(temp, 1.0f)-style magnitude assignment with explicit control flow: assign mag from temp, clamp mag down to 1.0f when mag > 1.0f, then multiply by da->xF0.
Removed an extra PAD_STACK(4) before Vec3 sp30 in fn_8012E110, restoring the target stack layout for the first Vec3.
Introduced a scoped float x4 temporary for fp->mv.ys.specialhi.x4 so the value is converted once and reused in the it_802B28C8 call.
Verified with ninja progress, git diff --check, and cargo run -p melee-issues; melee-issues reported only existing issues outside the touched file.
smart_moves: Used explicit if-based clamp code instead of a macro/expression form because the target needed the branch shape, not just equivalent behavior.
Recognized that repeated use of fp->mv.ys.specialhi.x4 caused different float conversion behavior; introducing a local temporary matched the target's convert-once-and-reuse pattern.
Removed artificial padding when it moved a stack object away from the expected offset, showing stack layout was checked against target offsets.
Scoped the temporary x4 locally around the it_802B28C8 call, minimizing source churn while shaping codegen.
Reported translation-unit-level progress and exact byte/function counts, making the matching result easy to audit.
decomp_lessons: Behaviorally similar clamp expressions can compile to different branch shapes; for matching, prefer the source form that reproduces target control flow.
Macros such as MAX may not be suitable when the target has explicit compare-and-assign codegen.
Do not keep PAD_STACK entries by inertia; verify whether they place locals at the target stack offsets.
Repeated struct field reads in argument expressions can introduce duplicate conversions or loads; a local temporary can force reuse.
Small source-shape fixes can finish near-matching functions without changing apparent gameplay behavior.
naming_conventions: Yoshi fighter functions use the ftYs_ prefix, as in ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C and ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C_outline.
Unidentified/local helper symbols may remain address-style names, as in fn_8012E110.
Yoshi-specific fighter state fields are accessed through fp->fv.ys and fp->mv.ys.specialhi.
Data-attribute fields remain offset-style names such as da->xEC, da->xF0, da->xF4, da->x108, da->x10C, and da->x110 where semantic names are not established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Explicitly wrote the cap-at-one branch as if (mag > 1.0f) { mag = 1.0f; } to match target control flow.
Removed PAD_STACK(4) so Vec3 sp30 occupied the target stack slot, described as r1+0x30 instead of r1+0x34.
Introduced float x4 = fp->mv.ys.specialhi.x4 before it_802B28C8 so the egg throw frame counter was converted to float once and reused.
Kept the it_802B28C8 argument math as x4 * da->x110 + da->x10C, x4 to preserve the desired reuse pattern.
Confirmed the full translation unit matched with ninja progress: ftYs_SpecialHi 100.0%, 14/14 functions, 1836/1836 code bytes.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer feedback was available; conclusions are based on the PR body and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find other near-matching fighter functions using MAX or MIN where explicit clamp control flow may be needed.
Search for PAD_STACK near Vec3 locals in fighter code to verify stack slot offsets.
Search for repeated fp->mv or fp->fv field expressions passed as multiple float arguments where a scoped temporary could avoid duplicate conversions.
Compare other Yoshi SpecialHi or egg/item throw routines that call it_802B28C8 for similar x4 temporary patterns.
